name: Phase 1 — Solana Zig SDK Off-chain Client Core

userStories.title: Pubkey type with base58 encoding/decoding
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want Pubkey type with 32-byte fixed layout and base58 encode/decode so that I can correctly represent and transmit Solana addresses.
acceptanceCriteria: Pubkey is a 32-byte fixed array type
Supports decoding from base58 string to Pubkey
Supports encoding from Pubkey to base58 string
Handles leading-zero scenarios correctly
Invalid base58 input returns clear error
Oracle vector: non-zero pubkey encode/decode matches Rust SDK output

userStories.title: Signature type with base58 encoding/decoding
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want Signature type with 64-byte fixed layout and base58 encode/decode so that I can correctly represent and verify transaction signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: Signature is a 64-byte fixed array type
Supports base58 encode/decode
Supports default value (all zeros) for unsigned
Invalid input returns clear error

userStories.title: Hash type with base58 encoding/decoding
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want Hash type with 32-byte fixed layout and base58 encode/decode so that I can correctly represent blockhash and other hash values.
acceptanceCriteria: Hash is a 32-byte fixed array type
Supports base58 encode/decode
Supports construction from raw byte array

userStories.title: Keypair generation and Ed25519 signing
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want Keypair with generation, seed recovery, and signing so that I can sign transactions in Zig.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports random Keypair generation
Supports recovery from 64-byte seed
Supports Ed25519 signing
Supports signature verification
Oracle vector: deterministic seed signature matches Rust SDK output

userStories.title: shortvec encode/decode
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want shortvec encode/decode so that variable-length fields in Message serialization are correctly encoded.
acceptanceCriteria: Implements shortvec encode/decode
Behavior matches solana-short-vec 3.2.0
Covers boundary value tests (0, 127, 128, 16383, 16384, etc.)

userStories.title: Instruction and AccountMeta structs
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want Instruction and AccountMeta structs so that I can construct transaction instructions.
acceptanceCriteria: Instruction contains program_id, accounts, data fields
AccountMeta contains pubkey, is_signer, is_writable fields
Field layout aligns with Rust SDK

userStories.title: Legacy Message compilation and serialization
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want to build and serialize legacy Message so that I can construct traditional format transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports compiling legacy Message from Instructions + payer + blockhash
Serialization output is byte-identical to Rust SDK
Supports deserialization
Correctly orders account keys (signer-writable > signer-readonly > non-signer-writable > non-signer-readonly)
Oracle vector: multi-instruction legacy message serialization matches Rust output

userStories.title: V0 Message compilation and serialization
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want to build and serialize v0 Message with Address Lookup Table support so that I can use the new transaction format.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports v0 Message compilation with ALT references
Serialization output is byte-identical to Rust SDK
Supports deserialization
MessageAddressTableLookup struct is correct
Oracle vector: v0 message with ALT serialization matches Rust output

userStories.title: VersionedTransaction sign, verify, serialize, deserialize
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want VersionedTransaction with sign/verify/serialize/deserialize so that I can complete the full transaction workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports building VersionedTransaction for both legacy and v0 Message
Supports single-signer and multi-signer
sign then verify passes
Serialize/deserialize roundtrip is consistent
Oracle vector: full Transaction serialize (signatures + message) matches Rust output

userStories.title: RPC Transport abstraction and HTTP client
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want pluggable RPC transport so that I can mock in tests and use HTTP in production.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines transport interface (send JSON-RPC request, return response)
Implements default HTTP transport based on std.http.Client
Supports mock transport injection for testing
Request/response follows JSON-RPC 2.0 spec

userStories.title: getLatestBlockhash RPC method
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want to call getLatestBlockhash so that I can get the latest blockhash for transaction construction.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns blockhash (Hash type) and lastValidBlockHeight
Supports commitment parameter (processed/confirmed/finalized)
RPC errors return structured error info
Mock tests cover success and error paths

userStories.title: getAccountInfo RPC method
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want to call getAccountInfo so that I can read on-chain account data.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns AccountInfo (lamports, owner, data, executable, rentEpoch)
Returns null when account does not exist
Supports encoding parameter (base64)
Mock tests cover success, not-found, and error paths

userStories.title: getBalance RPC method
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want to call getBalance so that I can query account balance.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns lamports balance (u64)
Supports commitment parameter
Mock tests cover success and error paths

userStories.title: simulateTransaction RPC method
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want to call simulateTransaction so that I can pre-validate transactions before sending.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts base64-encoded serialized transaction
Returns simulation result (err, logs, unitsConsumed)
Returns specific error info on simulation failure
Mock tests cover success and failure paths

userStories.title: sendTransaction RPC method
userStories.description: As a Zig developer, I want to call sendTransaction so that I can submit signed transactions to the network.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepts base64-encoded serialized transaction
Returns transaction signature (Signature type)
Supports optional parameters like skipPreflight
RPC errors return structured error info
Mock tests cover success and error paths

userStories.title: Oracle vector cross-reference test suite
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want oracle vector cross-reference tests covering critical paths so that Zig implementation byte-compatibility with Rust SDK can be regression-tested.
acceptanceCriteria: Non-zero pubkey (including leading-zero scenarios) vectors pass
Keypair sign -> Signature (deterministic seed) vectors pass
Legacy message serialize (multi-instruction) vectors pass
V0 message serialize (with ALT) vectors pass
Full Transaction serialize (signatures + message) vectors pass
Vector data embedded as static files or comptime, no external service dependency

userStories.title: Devnet E2E acceptance test path
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want Devnet end-to-end tests so that I can verify the complete transaction flow on a real network.
acceptanceCriteria: zig build devnet-e2e runs the end-to-end flow
Covers construct -> sign -> simulate -> send -> confirm flow
Gated by SOLANA_RPC_URL environment variable, skips when not set
Test output includes key info like transaction signatures for documentation

userStories.title: Benchmark baseline establishment
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want serialization/deserialization benchmark baselines so that future optimizations have data for comparison.
acceptanceCriteria: zig build bench runs benchmarks
Covers Pubkey base58 encode/decode, Message serialization, Transaction serialize/deserialize
Output includes quantitative metrics (ops/sec or ns/op)
Results can be recorded for comparison (no requirement to beat Rust, just establish baseline)
